These are classic signs of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), especially given his long smoking history.
COPD includes emphysema and chronic bronchitis, which can cause shortness of breath and fatigue.
The weak pulses might be due to reduced cardiac output if there's cor pulmonale, which can develop in advanced COPD.

I should also consider other possibilities like asthma, but his age and smoking history make COPD more likely.
Chronic bronchitis would present with cough and mucus, which isn't mentioned, but sometimes patients don't report all symptoms.
Emphysema typically presents with breathlessness on exertion, which aligns with his complaints.
So, after considering all these factors, the most likely diagnosis is Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease.
